# 🚨 MANDATORY: Team Memory System 🚨

A shared team memory stores critical gotchas, conventions, and debugging insights across sessions.

## REQUIRED: /recall BEFORE Starting Any Task

You MUST run /recall at the start of every task to check for relevant prior knowledge. Skipping this risks repeating known mistakes and wasting time on already-solved problems.

```
/recall "<topic or keyword relevant to your task>"
```

Examples:
- Working on Stripe integration? → /recall "Stripe"
- Fixing a database query? → /recall "database" or /recall "Drizzle"
- Debugging a test? → /recall "testing patterns"
- Working on team creation? → /recall "team" or /recall "createId"
- Touching waivers? → /recall "waiver"

**Do NOT skip this step.** Past sessions have stored critical gotchas that will save significant debugging time.

## REQUIRED: /remember After Discoveries

When you discover something useful — a gotcha, root cause, convention, or debugging insight — you MUST save it immediately with /remember. Do not wait until the end of the session.

```
/remember "<what you learned>" --category=gotcha|convention|debugging|architecture|workflow --priority=critical|moderate|ephemeral
```

## Summary

- **BEFORE work**: /recall to load relevant memories (MANDATORY)
- **DURING work**: /remember as soon as you discover something worth saving (MANDATORY)
- Failure to use team memory means the team loses knowledge and repeats mistakes.

## deepwiki
DeepWiki MCP provides AI-powered documentation for GitHub repositories.

Available tools:
- read_wiki_structure: Get a list of documentation topics for a repository
- read_wiki_contents: View full documentation about a repository
- ask_question: Ask any question about a repository and get an AI-powered answer
- list_available_repos: List your available repositories (private mode only)
- generate_wiki: Generate a codebase wiki for a repository — only use when explicitly requested by the user (private mode only)
- devin_knowledge_manage: Manage Devin knowledge notes and suggestions — list, search, get, create, update, delete notes, view folder structure, list/view/dismiss knowledge suggestions (private mode only)
- devin_playbook_manage: Manage Devin playbooks — list, get, create, update, delete (private mode only)
- devin_schedule_manage: Manage scheduled Devin sessions — list, get, create, update, delete (private mode only)
- devin_session_create: Create one or more child Devin sessions (private mode only)
- devin_session_interact: Manage a Devin session — get status, send messages, sleep/terminate/archive, read messages & attachments, manage tags (private mode only)
- devin_session_events: Inspect session events — list summaries, fetch full details, or search event contents (private mode only)
- devin_session_search: Search and filter Devin sessions (private mode only)
- list_integrations: List all native integrations and MCP servers with their status and settings URLs (private mode only)

Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Base directory for this skill: /Users/zacjones/Documents/02.Areas/wodsmith/thewodapp-2/.claude/skills/my-pr-comments

Get the current pr number:

```bash
gh pr view --json "url" | jq '.url'
```

Now you can run this command to get all the pr comments in JSON

```bash
gh api repos/corpaxe/{reponame}/pulls/{prnumber}/comments --paginate --jq '.[] | {file: .path, line: .line, body: .body, author: .user.login}'
```

## Example:

```bash
gh pr view --json "url" | jq '.url'

# outputs
"https://github.com/corpaxe/External.Frontend/pull/2972"

gh api   repos/corpaxe/External.Frontend/pulls/2976/comments | jq '.[] | {file: .path, line: .line, body: .body, author: .user.login}'
```

1	/**
2	 * WeTime Video Resolver
3	 *
4	 * WeTime share URLs (`wetime.io/preview/ID`) don't expose a direct media URL
5	 * via the share token — the ID maps to a CloudFront-hosted MP4 only after the
6	 * preview page is rendered. This server function fetches the preview HTML and
7	 * extracts the `<source src="...mp4">` so we can play it in a native <video>
8	 * element instead of iframing the full WeTime chrome.
9	 */
10	
11	import { createServerFn } from "@tanstack/react-start"
12	import { z } from "zod"
13	import { getSessionFromCookie } from "@/utils/auth"
14	
15	const inputSchema = z.object({
16	  videoId: z
17	    .string()
18	    .min(1)
19	    .max(64)
20	    .regex(/^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$/, "Invalid WeTime video id"),
21	})
22	
23	export const resolveWeTimeVideoUrlFn = createServerFn({ method: "GET" })
24	  .inputValidator((input: unknown) => inputSchema.parse(input))
25	  .handler(async ({ data }): Promise<{ videoUrl: string }> => {
26	    const session = await getSessionFromCookie()
27	    if (!session?.userId) {
28	      throw new Error("Not authenticated")
29	    }
30	
31	    const previewUrl = `https://wetime.io/preview/${data.videoId}`
32	    const res = await fetch(previewUrl, {
33	      headers: {
34	        "User-Agent":
35	          "Mozilla/5.0 (compatible; WodsmithBot/1.0; +https://wodsmith.com)",
36	      },
37	    })
38	    if (!res.ok) {
39	      throw new Error(`WeTime preview fetch failed: ${res.status}`)
40	    }
41	
42	    const html = await res.text()
43	    const match = html.match(/<source\s+src="([^"]+\.mp4[^"]*)"/i)
44	    if (!match?.[1]) {
45	      throw new Error("Could not locate video source in WeTime preview")
46	    }
47	
48	    return { videoUrl: match[1] }
49	  })
50

1	/**
2	 * Video URL Validation Schema
3	 *
4	 * Validates video URLs for online competition submissions.
5	 * Supports YouTube, Vimeo, and WodProof URL formats with video ID extraction.
6	 */
7	
8	import { z } from "zod"
9	
10	/**
11	 * Supported video platforms
12	 */
13	export const VIDEO_PLATFORMS = [
14	  "youtube",
15	  "vimeo",
16	  "wodproof",
17	  "wetime",
18	] as const
19	export type VideoPlatform = (typeof VIDEO_PLATFORMS)[number]
20	
21	/**
22	 * Result of parsing a video URL
23	 */
24	export interface ParsedVideoUrl {
25	  platform: VideoPlatform
26	  videoId: string
27	  originalUrl: string
28	  embedUrl: string
29	  thumbnailUrl: string
30	  /** Privacy hash for unlisted Vimeo videos */
31	  privacyHash?: string
32	  /** Whether this platform supports inline embedding (iframe/player) */
33	  supportsEmbed: boolean
34	}
35	
36	/**
37	 * YouTube URL patterns:
38	 * - youtube.com/watch?v=VIDEO_ID
39	 * - youtube.com/watch?v=VIDEO_ID&t=123s (with timestamp)
40	 * - youtu.be/VIDEO_ID
41	 * - youtu.be/VIDEO_ID?t=123 (with timestamp)
42	 * - youtube.com/embed/VIDEO_ID
43	 * - youtube.com/v/VIDEO_ID
44	 * - youtube.com/shorts/VIDEO_ID
45	 * - m.youtube.com/watch?v=VIDEO_ID (mobile)
46	 * - www.youtube.com/watch?v=VIDEO_ID
47	 */
48	const YOUTUBE_PATTERNS = [
49	  // Standard watch URL
50	  /^(?:https?:\/\/)?(?:www\.|m\.)?youtube\.com\/watch\?(?:.*&)?v=([a-zA-Z0-9_-]{11})(?:&.*)?$/,
51	  // Short URL
52	  /^(?:https?:\/\/)?youtu\.be\/([a-zA-Z0-9_-]{11})(?:\?.*)?$/,
53	  // Embed URL
54	  /^(?:https?:\/\/)?(?:www\.)?youtube\.com\/embed\/([a-zA-Z0-9_-]{11})(?:\?.*)?$/,
55	  // Old-style /v/ URL
56	  /^(?:https?:\/\/)?(?:www\.)?youtube\.com\/v\/([a-zA-Z0-9_-]{11})(?:\?.*)?$/,
57	  // Shorts URL
58	  /^(?:https?:\/\/)?(?:www\.)?youtube\.com\/shorts\/([a-zA-Z0-9_-]{11})(?:\?.*)?$/,
59	] as const
60	
61	/**
62	 * Vimeo URL patterns:
63	 * - vimeo.com/VIDEO_ID
64	 * - vimeo.com/VIDEO_ID?share=copy (with params)
65	 * - vimeo.com/VIDEO_ID/HASH (unlisted videos with privacy hash)
66	 * - player.vimeo.com/video/VIDEO_ID
67	 * - player.vimeo.com/video/VIDEO_ID?h=HASH (unlisted embed)
68	 * - www.vimeo.com/VIDEO_ID
69	 * - vimeo.com/channels/CHANNEL/VIDEO_ID
70	 * - vimeo.com/groups/GROUP/videos/VIDEO_ID
71	 */
72	const VIMEO_PATTERNS = [
73	  // Unlisted URL with privacy hash (must be before standard pattern)
74	  /^(?:https?:\/\/)?(?:www\.)?vimeo\.com\/(\d+)\/([a-zA-Z0-9]+)(?:\?.*)?$/,
75	  // Standard URL
76	  /^(?:https?:\/\/)?(?:www\.)?vimeo\.com\/(\d+)(?:\?.*)?$/,
77	  // Player embed URL with hash
78	  /^(?:https?:\/\/)?player\.vimeo\.com\/video\/(\d+)\?(?:.*&)?h=([a-zA-Z0-9]+)(?:&.*)?$/,
79	  // Player embed URL without hash
80	  /^(?:https?:\/\/)?player\.vimeo\.com\/video\/(\d+)(?:\?.*)?$/,
81	  // Channel video URL
82	  /^(?:https?:\/\/)?(?:www\.)?vimeo\.com\/channels\/[^/]+\/(\d+)(?:\?.*)?$/,
83	  // Group video URL
84	  /^(?:https?:\/\/)?(?:www\.)?vimeo\.com\/groups\/[^/]+\/videos\/(\d+)(?:\?.*)?$/,
85	] as const
86	
87	/**
88	 * WodProof URL patterns:
89	 *
90	 * WodProof videos are shared as cloud links from the mobile app.
91	 * The cloud URL format is: https://wodproofapp.com/cloud/?v=VIDEO_ID
92	 * The actual MP4 is hosted at: https://s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/wodproof-cloud/VIDEO_ID.mp4
93	 *
94	 * We extract the video ID from the `v` query parameter and construct
95	 * the S3 URL for direct <video> element playback.
96	 *
97	 * Known domain patterns:
98	 * - wodproofapp.com/cloud/?v=ID (cloud share links)
99	 * - wodproofapp.com/* (other paths)
100	 * - *.wodproof.com/* (any subdomain)
101	 */
102	const WODPROOF_PATTERNS = [
103	  // wodproofapp.com with any path (main domain, www only)
104	  /^(?:https?:\/\/)?(?:www\.)?wodproofapp\.com\/(.+)$/,
105	  // wodproof.com (alternate domain) with path
106	  /^(?:https?:\/\/)?(?:www\.)?wodproof\.com\/(.+)$/,
107	] as const
108	
109	/**
110	 * WeTime URL patterns:
111	 *
112	 * WeTime is a competition timing/video-proof service. Shareable links look like
113	 * https://wetime.io/preview/ID where ID is a short alphanumeric token.
114	 * The preview page itself embeds a <video> with a CloudFront-hosted MP4, but
115	 * the ID → MP4 mapping requires fetching the page — we iframe the preview URL
116	 * directly instead. CSP `frame-ancestors 'self' *` permits cross-origin framing.
117	 */
118	const WETIME_PATTERNS = [
119	  // Canonical share URL: wetime.io/preview/ID
120	  /^(?:https?:\/\/)?(?:www\.)?wetime\.io\/preview\/([a-zA-Z0-9_-]+)(?:[/?#].*)?$/,
121	] as const
122	
123	/**
124	 * Extract the video ID from a WodProof URL.
125	 * For cloud URLs (wodproofapp.com/cloud/?v=ID), extracts the `v` query parameter.
126	 * For other WodProof URLs, falls back to the path as an identifier.
127	 */
128	function extractWodProofId(url: string): string | null {
129	  for (const pattern of WODPROOF_PATTERNS) {
130	    const match = url.match(pattern)
131	    if (match) {
132	      // Try to extract `v` query parameter from cloud URLs
133	      try {
134	        const parsed = new URL(
135	          url.startsWith("http") ? url : `https://${url}`,
136	        )
137	        const videoId = parsed.searchParams.get("v")
138	        if (videoId) {
139	          return videoId
140	        }
141	      } catch {
142	        // Fall through to path-based extraction
143	      }
144	
145	      // Use the last captured group (the path) as the ID
146	      const path = match[match.length - 1]
147	      if (path && path.length > 0) {
148	        return path.replace(/[?#].*$/, "") // strip query/hash
149	      }
150	    }
151	  }
152	  return null
153	}
154	
155	/**
156	 * Construct the direct S3 MP4 URL for a WodProof video.
157	 * WodProof stores videos at: https://s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/wodproof-cloud/{videoId}.mp4
158	 */
159	export function getWodProofVideoUrl(videoId: string): string {
160	  return `https://s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/wodproof-cloud/${videoId}.mp4`
161	}
162	
163	/**
164	 * Extract the video ID from a WeTime preview URL.
165	 */
166	function extractWeTimeId(url: string): string | null {
167	  for (const pattern of WETIME_PATTERNS) {
168	    const match = url.match(pattern)
169	    if (match?.[1]) {
170	      return match[1]
171	    }
172	  }
173	  return null
174	}
175	
176	/**
177	 * Extract video ID from YouTube URL
178	 */
179	function extractYouTubeId(url: string): string | null {
180	  for (const pattern of YOUTUBE_PATTERNS) {
181	    const match = url.match(pattern)
182	    if (match?.[1]) {
183	      return match[1]
184	    }
185	  }
186	  return null
187	}
188	
189	/**
190	 * Extract video ID and optional privacy hash from Vimeo URL
191	 */
192	function extractVimeoId(url: string): { id: string; hash?: string } | null {
193	  for (const pattern of VIMEO_PATTERNS) {
194	    const match = url.match(pattern)
195	    if (match?.[1]) {
196	      return {
197	        id: match[1],
198	        hash: match[2] || undefined,
199	      }
200	    }
201	  }
202	  return null
203	}
204	
205	/**
206	 * Parse a video URL and extract platform and video ID
207	 * Returns null if URL is not from a supported platform
208	 */
209	export function parseVideoUrl(url: string): ParsedVideoUrl | null {
210	  const trimmedUrl = url.trim()
211	
212	  // Try YouTube first
213	  const youtubeId = extractYouTubeId(trimmedUrl)
214	  if (youtubeId) {
215	    return {
216	      platform: "youtube",
217	      videoId: youtubeId,
218	      originalUrl: trimmedUrl,
219	      embedUrl: `https://www.youtube.com/embed/${youtubeId}`,
220	      thumbnailUrl: `https://img.youtube.com/vi/${youtubeId}/maxresdefault.jpg`,
221	      supportsEmbed: true,
222	    }
223	  }
224	
225	  // Try Vimeo
226	  const vimeoResult = extractVimeoId(trimmedUrl)
227	  if (vimeoResult) {
228	    // For unlisted videos, include the privacy hash in the embed URL
229	    const embedUrl = vimeoResult.hash
230	      ? `https://player.vimeo.com/video/${vimeoResult.id}?h=${vimeoResult.hash}`
231	      : `https://player.vimeo.com/video/${vimeoResult.id}`
232	    return {
233	      platform: "vimeo",
234	      videoId: vimeoResult.id,
235	      originalUrl: trimmedUrl,
236	      embedUrl,
237	      // Vimeo thumbnails require API access, use placeholder
238	      thumbnailUrl: `https://vumbnail.com/${vimeoResult.id}.jpg`,
239	      privacyHash: vimeoResult.hash,
240	      supportsEmbed: true,
241	    }
242	  }
243	
244	  // Try WodProof
245	  const wodproofId = extractWodProofId(trimmedUrl)
246	  if (wodproofId) {
247	    return {
248	      platform: "wodproof",
249	      videoId: wodproofId,
250	      originalUrl: trimmedUrl,
251	      embedUrl: getWodProofVideoUrl(wodproofId),
252	      thumbnailUrl: "",
253	      supportsEmbed: true,
254	    }
255	  }
256	
257	  // Try WeTime
258	  const wetimeId = extractWeTimeId(trimmedUrl)
259	  if (wetimeId) {
260	    return {
261	      platform: "wetime",
262	      videoId: wetimeId,
263	      originalUrl: trimmedUrl,
264	      embedUrl: `https://wetime.io/preview/${wetimeId}`,
265	      thumbnailUrl: "",
266	      supportsEmbed: true,
267	    }
268	  }
269	
270	  return null
271	}
272	
273	/**
274	 * Check if a URL is from a supported video platform
275	 */
276	export function isSupportedVideoUrl(url: string): boolean {
277	  return parseVideoUrl(url) !== null
278	}
279	
280	/**
281	 * Get a human-readable list of supported platforms
282	 */
283	export function getSupportedPlatformsText(): string {
284	  return "YouTube, Vimeo, WodProof, or WeTime"
285	}
286	
287	/**
288	 * Error messages for video URL validation
289	 */
290	export const VIDEO_URL_ERRORS = {
291	  INVALID_URL: "Please enter a valid URL",
292	  UNSUPPORTED_PLATFORM: `Please use a video from ${getSupportedPlatformsText()}`,
293	  EMPTY_URL: "Video URL is required",
294	  EXTRACT_FAILED:
295	    "Could not extract video ID from URL. Please check the URL format.",
296	} as const
297	
298	/**
299	 * Zod schema for validating video URLs
300	 *
301	 * Usage:
302	 * ```typescript
303	 * const result = videoUrlSchema.safeParse(url)
304	 * if (result.success) {
305	 *   console.log(result.data) // ParsedVideoUrl
306	 * }
307	 * ```
308	 */
309	export const videoUrlSchema = z
310	  .string()
311	  .min(1, VIDEO_URL_ERRORS.EMPTY_URL)
312	  .url(VIDEO_URL_ERRORS.INVALID_URL)
313	  .transform((url, ctx) => {
314	    const parsed = parseVideoUrl(url)
315	    if (!parsed) {
316	      ctx.addIssue({
317	        code: z.ZodIssueCode.custom,
318	        message: VIDEO_URL_ERRORS.UNSUPPORTED_PLATFORM,
319	      })
320	      return z.NEVER
321	    }
322	    return parsed
323	  })
324	
325	/**
326	 * Zod schema for optional video URL
327	 * Returns ParsedVideoUrl if valid, undefined if empty
328	 */
329	export const optionalVideoUrlSchema = z
330	  .string()
331	  .optional()
332	  .nullable()
333	  .transform((url, ctx) => {
334	    // Allow empty/null values
335	    if (!url || url.trim() === "") {
336	      return undefined
337	    }
338	
339	    // Validate URL format
340	    try {
341	      new URL(url)
342	    } catch {
343	      ctx.addIssue({
344	        code: z.ZodIssueCode.custom,
345	        message: VIDEO_URL_ERRORS.INVALID_URL,
346	      })
347	      return z.NEVER
348	    }
349	
350	    // Parse and validate platform
351	    const parsed = parseVideoUrl(url)
352	    if (!parsed) {
353	      ctx.addIssue({
354	        code: z.ZodIssueCode.custom,
355	        message: VIDEO_URL_ERRORS.UNSUPPORTED_PLATFORM,
356	      })
357	      return z.NEVER
358	    }
359	
360	    return parsed
361	  })
362	
363	/**
364	 * Simple string schema for video URL validation (returns string, not parsed object)
365	 * Use this when you just need to validate the URL but store the original string
366	 */
367	export const videoUrlStringSchema = z
368	  .string()
369	  .min(1, VIDEO_URL_ERRORS.EMPTY_URL)
370	  .url(VIDEO_URL_ERRORS.INVALID_URL)
371	  .refine((url) => isSupportedVideoUrl(url), {
372	    message: VIDEO_URL_ERRORS.UNSUPPORTED_PLATFORM,
373	  })
374	
375	/**
376	 * Optional string schema for video URL validation
377	 * Returns the original string if valid, undefined if empty
378	 */
379	export const optionalVideoUrlStringSchema = z
380	  .string()
381	  .optional()
382	  .nullable()
383	  .transform((url, ctx) => {
384	    // Allow empty/null values
385	    if (!url || url.trim() === "") {
386	      return undefined
387	    }
388	
389	    // Validate URL format
390	    try {
391	      new URL(url)
392	    } catch {
393	      ctx.addIssue({
394	        code: z.ZodIssueCode.custom,
395	        message: VIDEO_URL_ERRORS.INVALID_URL,
396	      })
397	      return z.NEVER
398	    }
399	
400	    // Validate it's from a supported platform
401	    if (!isSupportedVideoUrl(url)) {
402	      ctx.addIssue({
403	        code: z.ZodIssueCode.custom,
404	        message: VIDEO_URL_ERRORS.UNSUPPORTED_PLATFORM,
405	      })
406	      return z.NEVER
407	    }
408	
409	    return url
410	  })
411	
412	// Type exports
413	export type VideoUrlInput = z.input<typeof videoUrlSchema>
414	export type VideoUrlOutput = z.output<typeof videoUrlSchema>
415	export type OptionalVideoUrlOutput = z.output<typeof optionalVideoUrlSchema>

file.content:
### Supported Video Platforms

YouTube, Vimeo, WodProof, and WeTime URLs are validated and parsed by [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/schemas/video-url.ts#parseVideoUrl]].

YouTube and Vimeo use iframe embeds with their JS player APIs, which drive the review UI's seek-to-note and timestamp-capture features. WodProof cloud URLs (`wodproofapp.com/cloud/?v=ID`) are resolved to direct S3 MP4 URLs via [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/schemas/video-url.ts#getWodProofVideoUrl]] and rendered with a native `<video>` element in both the athlete-facing [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/video-embed.tsx#VideoEmbed]] and the interactive [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/compete/video-player-embed.tsx#VideoPlayerEmbed]] components. WeTime share links (`wetime.io/preview/ID`) don't expose a direct media URL from the share token, so [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/wetime-resolve-fns.ts#resolveWeTimeVideoUrlFn]] fetches the preview HTML on the server, extracts the `<source>` CloudFront MP4, and both embed components play it in a native `<video>` (the iframe-the-whole-page approach was rejected because WeTime's preview page wraps the video in heavy chrome, breaking the 16:9 layout). Because the result is a native video element, WeTime inherits full interactive-playback support (seek, `getCurrentTime`) for reviewer timestamp capture.

### Multi-Division Submission

Athletes registered in multiple divisions see a division picker on the submission form. Single-division athletes see a static badge.
